newsSince: Material positive. KYEC raised FY2026 capex from NT$39.4B to NT$50B (record), targeting CoWoS-package, custom-ASIC and next-gen-memory testers; its Singapore (Ang Mo Kio) test fab went live May 22, 2026; management frames testing as its own bottleneck (AI-chip power 3kW->8kW lengthens test cycles) with custom-ASIC demand from AWS/Google/Meta/Microsoft broadening beyond NVDA. Stock +13.2% since June 9.

sources.label: AI chip testing demand transforms KYEC (TVBS)
KYEC ramps Singapore expansion amid AI test surge (DigiTimes)

verdicts.rankRationale: Fresh evidence strengthens the OSAT/test-bottleneck thesis: record NT$50B capex (up from NT$39.4B) for CoWoS/ASIC/memory testers, Singapore fab live May 22, and longer AI-chip test cycles (3kW->8kW) turning test capacity into its own choke-point, with AWS/Google/Meta/MSFT ASICs broadening demand beyond Nvidia. Directly raises node-criticality vs peers.
verdicts.bull: Test-as-bottleneck confirmed; record capex, Singapore capacity live, custom-ASIC customer diversification beyond NVDA; only +23% YTD.
verdicts.neutral: High-conviction but capex ramp must convert to utilization and pricing.
verdicts.bear: Capex-heavy at cycle peak; utilization sensitive to AI-chip volume timing.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: CoWoS/custom-ASIC test capacity ramp + Singapore fab utilization.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Heavy capex outrunning utilization if AI-chip volumes slip.
